cohort_breakdown: 10 categories x {ZS, N=2, N=10, N=30}; 1 seed at ZS, 3 seeds at trained N. 90 trained cells + 10 ZS cells. Cumulative v0.3-tier-a cohort: 240 rows across 24 cats (Tier A complete).
verdict_summary: Stage3 verdict (cumulative cohort 240 rows): 168 keep, 45 noop, 24 new_baseline (ZS), 3 discard. Stage3 alone: 81 keep, 9 noop, 0 discard, 10 new_baseline. The 3 discard cells remain mvtec/transistor N=30 from stage1 — the only discard cluster across all of Tier A.
held_out_prediction_result: RULE PASSES per locked-in stage3 criteria (2 of 2 predictions met). Detailed: (1) Spearman rank correlation between ZS-AUROC and BEST_LIFT across all 24 cats: rho = -0.7782, one-sided p = 0.000004; passes locked-in threshold (rho<=-0.6 with p<0.01) by a strong margin. (2) N=2 captures >=80% of best-lift on 9 of 10 stage3 cats; passes locked-in threshold (>=6 of 10) decisively. The 10th cat (mvtec/leather) had ZS=0.999 and best_lift=+0.000 — there was effectively no lift to capture, not a knee failure.
diagnosis: The lift~(1-ZS_AUROC) rule is confirmed across the full Tier A cohort of 24 categories with held-out testing at every stage. The cumulative Spearman correlation rho=-0.778 is robust: stage-by-stage decomposition gives rho=-0.667 (stage1, n=8), rho=-0.943 (stage2, n=6), rho=-0.644 (stage3, n=10). Stage3 was the weakest of the three but still passed the locked-in threshold. The slight weakening in stage3 traces to two cats (mvtec/grid at ZS=0.778 with lift=+0.194; mvtec/zipper at ZS=0.629 with lift=+0.312) where lift exceeded what a strict linear interpretation of the rule would predict. These cats follow the qualitative direction (more lift than at-ceiling cats) but with greater magnitude than ZS-band alone explains. The N=2 knee shape held in 18 of 24 cats overall, including 9 of 10 stage3 cats — the knee finding generalizes across the full Tier A. The originally pre-registered distinctive-vs-subtle taxonomy remains falsified (mean lift: stage1 distinctive=+0.031, subtle=+0.218; the inversion held throughout).
recommendation: halt: done — pass4 Tier A coverage is complete. The locked-in rule passes both held-out tests. Three actionable next directions, in priority order: (a) Multi-model generalization on a subset of Tier A cats — Qwen2-VL or Idefics3 on 5-8 cats spanning the full ZS range. This is the highest-ROI follow-up because it converts a Gemma-specific finding into a generative-VLM property claim. Compute estimate: 4-6h trained + 1h ZS. (b) Recipe stability check — vary rank, learning rate, epochs on a subset of cats to defend against the 'rule is recipe-specific' attack. Compute estimate: 4-6h. (c) ICL extension — run ICL on the 4-5 highest-lift cats from stage1+2+3 (chewinggum, pcb4, capsule, pill, zipper) to characterize when ICL substitutes for FT. Compute estimate: 3-4h.
rationale: The cumulative Spearman result (rho=-0.778, p<10^-5) is robust across multiple held-out tests with predictions locked in BEFORE each test ran. The no-goalpost-moving clauses were honored at each stage: the per-cat ZS-band prediction failed on stage2 and was explicitly dropped before stage3 rather than retroactively softened. Stage3 used only two surviving predictions and both passed. The 3 discard cells remain confined to a single cat-N combination (transistor N=30), not scattered noise. Verdict status counts (168 keep + 45 noop + 24 new_baseline + 3 discard) on 240 rows show the recipe is broadly improving on ZS without systemic regressions. The stage3 outliers (grid, zipper) are honest residuals — the rule explains direction and most of magnitude, not all of it.
anti_goodhart_check: All 45 noop cells concentrate on either at-ceiling cats (carpet, leather, wood, grid where post-FT AUROC matches ZS within tolerance) or N=30 saturation on previously-lifted cats. No noop is a hidden regression. The single-cat discard cluster (transistor N=30) is consistent with the 'high-ZS cats with little headroom' subset — the recipe simply cannot help and at N=30 occasionally hurts. Three seeds per cell with mostly tight ranges (the largest seed-range was screw N=30 at [0.500, 0.856] from stage1, an honest documented training-instability cell).
next_action_sequence: Commit stage3 close-out + queue.json + autoresearch_state.json — final close-out for pass4.
Mark pass4 status=completed in queue.json with verdict_summary documenting the rule-passes outcome.

Optional follow-ups (in priority order): multi-model on subset, recipe stability, ICL extension. Each is independently valuable but the multi-model check is the single highest-ROI from a defensibility standpoint.
No additional Tier A work required — pass4 is closed.
